title: Generate k6 tests with Grafana Assistant

blocks.content:
Welcome to the learning path that shows you how to use **k6 Script Authoring** in Grafana Assistant to go from plain-language goals to runnable k6 scripts in Grafana Cloud. You stay in one place: describe the scenario, add telemetry or repository context when you have it, or paste something you already have—and you get structured JavaScript with checks, thresholds, and URL grouping that keeps cardinality under control.

This path complements journeys where you write k6 by hand. After you understand what a script should contain, Assistant helps you create, enrich, improve, convert, and hand off tests faster.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Explain how **k6 Script Authoring** differs from general Assistant use and when to open it
- Generate a new k6 script from a prompt, including smoke, load, stress, spike, or soak style goals
- Use metrics, logs, and traces plus optional OpenAPI or GitHub context to discover realistic endpoints
- Analyze and improve an existing k6 script for cardinality, checks, thresholds, and lifecycle issues
- Convert Postman collections or curl commands into k6
- Move a generated script into Grafana Cloud k6 and interpret a first run

## Before you begin

Before you use k6 Script Authoring, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- **Grafana Assistant** enabled on your stack. If you do not see the **Assistant** icon, ask your administrator to enable it.
- Familiarity with k6 basics (virtual users, stages, checks, and thresholds). If you are new to k6, complete [Run your first k6 performance test](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/run-first-k6-test/) first.
- Optional: metrics, logs, or traces in Grafana Cloud for the **Discover and generate** workflow, and an OpenAPI spec or GitHub repository URL if you want richer requests.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, refer to [Use k6 Script Authoring mode](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/author-run/k6-script-authoring-mode/) for access steps, example prompts, and known limitations.

## More to explore

Where it makes sense, milestones point you to related docs and paths so you can go deeper without leaving the main storyline.
